original_registry_entry.issuing_organisation: United States National Institute for Occupational Safety and Health
original_registry_entry.version: CDC/NIOSH HHE Program overview current 21 April 2026
original_registry_entry.jurisdiction: United States occupational-health programme; not a UAE regulatory standard
original_registry_entry.population: Workers, employers and unions concerned about potential workplace health hazards.
original_registry_entry.clinical_setting: Workplace hazard evaluation involving process observation, exposure information, worker health interviews, measurements and selected medical testing.
original_registry_entry.applicability_note: Exact only for structuring an exposure concern and distinguishing workplace evaluation from individual diagnosis. It does not establish causation, legal duty, UAE entitlement, testing, fitness or compensation.

exact_sections.heading: Overview — types of workplace health hazards and programme scope
exact_sections.locator: official HHE Program overview sections “Key points” and “Overview”
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports recording suspected physical, biological, chemical, ergonomic or psychosocial workplace hazard type and affected work context without establishing exposure or causation.

exact_sections.heading: Request and evaluation process — work, exposure, illness and privacy information
exact_sections.locator: official HHE request page sections on request, site visit, medical information and reports
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting work process, job, timing, reported exposure, symptoms, reports, measurements, privacy and occupational-evaluation context without generating tests, findings or recommendations.

title: NIOSH Health Hazard Evaluation (HHE) Program
publisher: United States National Institute for Occupational Safety and Health
jurisdiction: United States occupational-health programme; not a UAE regulatory standard
population: Workers, employers and unions concerned about potential workplace health hazards.
intended_setting: Workplace hazard evaluation involving process observation, exposure information, worker health interviews, measurements and selected medical testing.
